title: Model each lap as a set, then distill or blend
summary: Pit decisions depend on field context: rival tyre ages, lap-wide pressure, and who else looks near a stop.
rationale: Pit decisions depend on field context: rival tyre ages, lap-wide pressure, and who else looks near a stop. Set Transformer is designed for permutation-invariant set inputs and can model interactions among all drivers in the same lap snapshot without imposing arbitrary row order.
implementation_hint: For each `Year/Race/LapNumber`, build a set of driver feature vectors. Train a lightweight DeepSets/SetTransformer expert to output one score per driver in the set, using masking for varying field size. Use its OOF predictions directly in the ensemble or distill them into a GBM as an extra feature.
expected_effect: Could add genuinely new signal beyond hand-built cohort pressure features by learning higher-order field interactions automatically.
risk: Higher engineering cost and weaker single-model performance than GBMs if training is not well regularized; must avoid leakage from future laps in the set construction.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
